{{Quote|Are you familiar with the Blacksun Vaksai prototype? It's a new starship chassis that's as small as a standard light fighter but totally re-designed on the inside.|[[Rian Ry]]|<ref name="SWG" />}}
The '''Vaksai''' was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] model commonly employed by the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] crime syndicate during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. It was a [[TransGalMeg Industries, Incorporated]]-manufactured [[Kihraxz assault fighter|Kihraxz light fighter]] modified by Black Sun. A medium fighter, the Vaksai gave its [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] a good balance between firepower and maneuverability. While it was generally only used by Black Sun, one of these [[Starship/Legends|ships]] became the property of a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] who assisted the [[Civilian Protection Guild]] in the recapture of a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:VaksaiRihkxyrk-RECG.png|thumb|left|250px|A Rihkxyrk Assault Fighter flying in formation with two Vaksai starfighters]]
A [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]]-class [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the Vaksai model was a modified [[Kihraxz assault fighter|Kihraxz light fighter]] manufactured by [[TransGalMeg Industries, Incorporated]] that was later customized into its current state by the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] crime syndicate known as [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]]. A new ship of this model cost approximately 185,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] with used examples going as low as 115,000 credits, though its availability was restricted. Its maximum [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]] speed was 1,250 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] per [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]], and it possessed a cargo capacity of 95 [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] and two days' worth of supplies.<ref name="RECG" /> The Vaksai had over three times the maximum component capacity of the Kihraxz, allowing for heavier, more powerful [[Engine/Legends|engines]], weapons, capacitors, and other systems.<ref name="SWG" />

Standard armament was a single medium [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]], a [[Concussion missile launcher/Legends|concussion missile launcher]],<ref name="RECG" /> and a launcher for [[Warhead countermeasure|countermeasures]] against ordnance such as [[Missile/Legends|missiles]], although its armaments could be customized further to the owner's liking.<ref name="SWG" /> The Vaksai was equipped with [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]] and was manned by one individual, with no room for crew. The model was fitted with a Class 1 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]<ref name="RECG" /> and used a [[Navigation computer/Legends|flight computer]] as opposed to an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]]. The ship was equipped with a single [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] for the pilot and a [[Life support/Legends|life support]] system, allowing the pilot to be free from wearing a [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]]. [[Color/Legends|Color]] customization of the ship's [[Hull/Legends|hull]] was also possible.<ref name="SWG" />

==Role==
A medium starfighter, the Vaksai gave its pilot a middle ground between energy output and maneuverability, and firepower and speed.<ref name="SWG" />

==History==
[[File:KihraxzAssaultFighter-TCGEFH.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A Vaksai fighter]]
The Vaksai saw almost exclusive use by the Black Sun crime syndicate during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. However, a freelance [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] was given one of these fighters after assisting the [[Civilian Protection Guild]] with the recapture of a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] over the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. The [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] ''[[Vallent IX]]'' was stolen from the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] by a traitorous Alliance engineer who planned to deliver it to a group known as the Ghrag Mercenaries, one of the many enemies of the guild. [[Rian Ry]], the commander of the guild's [[Kashyyyk Space Station]], hired the spacer to find the corvette in the [[Realspace/Legends|space]] above Kashyyyk, destroy its Ghrag escort, and return it to [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the planet. The spacer was successful, and Rian Ry rewarded the being with the deed to a Vaksai starfighter.<ref name="SWG" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Vaksai's first appearance in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[Canon|continuity]] was in [[Star Wars Galaxies: Rage of the Wookiees|''Rage of the Wookiees'']], an expansion pack for ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'' developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and released on [[May 5]], [[2005]].<ref name="SWG" /> The fighter was later added to the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]] with the release of the ''[[Rebellion Era Campaign Guide]]'' on [[July 21]], [[2009]].<ref name="RECG" /> In ''Star Wars Galaxies'', the Vaksai was a reward for freelance pilots who assisted the Civilian Protection Guild in their recapture of a CR90 corvette in the space above Kashyyyk.<ref name="SWG" />

The reward from the quest was based on the player's specific pilot profession. [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] pilots received an [[Advanced X-wing fighter|advanced heavy]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]], while [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] pilots received an [[Emperor's Royal Guard TIE/IN starfighter]]. The player was able to receive at least two of these three ships if they changed pilot factions and returned later to redo the quest.<ref name="SWG" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Rage of the Wookiees]]'' {{1st}}
